Reads unprocessed feedback signals from the session store\n77\t    2. Analyzes patterns in the signals\n78\t    3. Proposes artifact updates via self-modification agent\n79\t    4. Surfaces proposals as canvas cards\n80\t    \"\"\"\n81\t\n82\t    def __init__(\n83\t        self,\n84\t        signal_threshold: int = 10,\n85\t        check_interval: int = 60,\n86\t    ):\n87\t        self.signal_threshold = signal_threshold\n88\t        self.check_interval = check_interval\n89\t        self.running = False\n90\t        self.task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n91\t        self.store = get_store()\n92\t        self.self_mod_agent = get_self_modification_agent()\n93\t\n94\t    async def analyze_signals(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n95\t        \"\"\"\n96\t        Analyze unprocessed feedback signals and generate proposals.\n97\t\n98\t        Args:\n99\t            limit: Maximum number of signals to process in one batch\n100\t\n101\t        Returns:\n102\t            List of proposals for artifact updates\n103\t        \"\"\"\n104\t        # Get unprocessed signals\n105\t        signals = await self.store.get_unprocessed_signals(limit=limit)\n106\t\n107\t        if not signals:\n108\t            logger.debug(\"No unprocessed signals to analyze\")\n109\t            return []\n110\t\n111\t        logger.info(f\"Analyzing {len(signals)} feedback signals\")\n112\t\n113\t        # Group signals by type\n114\t        signals_by_type = self._group_by_type(signals)\n115\t\n116\t        # Generate proposals for each signal type\n117\t        proposals = []\n118\t\n119\t        for signal_type, type_signals in signals_by_type.items():\n120\t            type_proposals = await self._analyze_signal_type(signal_type, type_signals)\n121\t            proposals.extend(type_proposals)\n122\t\n123\t        # Mark processed signals\n124\t        signal_ids = [s[\"signal_id\"] for s in signals]\n125\t        await self.store.mark_signals_processed(signal_ids)\n126\t\n127\t        logger.info(f\"Generated {len(proposals)} proposals from {len(signals)} signals\")\n128\t\n129\t        return proposals\n130\t\n131\t    def _group_by_type(self, signals: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:\n132\t        \"\"\"Group signals by type.\"\"\"\n133\t        grouped = {}\n134\t        for signal in signals:\n135\t            signal_type = signal[\"signal_type\"]\n136\t            if signal_type not in grouped:\n137\t                grouped[signal_type] = []\n138\t            grouped[signal_type].append(signal)\n139\t        return grouped\n140\t\n141\t    async def _analyze_signal_type(\n142\t        self,\n143\t        signal_type: str,\n144\t        signals: list[dict],\n145\t    ) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n146\t        \"\"\"Analyze signals of a specific type and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n147\t        proposals = []\n148\t\n149\t        # Extract unique session_ids from these signals\n150\t        session_ids = set(s.get(\"session_id\") for s in signals if s.get(\"session_id\"))\n151\t\n152\t        if signal_type == \"ack_speed\":\n153\t            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_ack_speed(signals, session_ids))\n154\t        elif signal_type == \"follow_up_pattern\":\n155\t            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_follow_up_patterns(signals, session_ids))\n156\t        elif signal_type == \"surface_switch\":\n157\t            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_surface_switches(signals, session_ids))\n158\t        elif signal_type == \"result_requery\":\n159\t            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_requeries(signals, session_ids))\n160\t        elif signal_type == \"result_skipped\":\n161\t            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_skipped_results(signals, session_ids))\n162\t        elif signal_type == \"topic_continuation\":\n163\t            # Topic continuation signals are informational, don't generate proposals\n164\t            pass\n165\t        elif signal_type == \"topic_switch\":\n166\t            # Topic switches are informational, don't generate proposals\n167\t            pass\n168\t\n169\t        return proposals\n170\t\n171\t    async def _analyze_ack_speed(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n172\t        \"\"\"\n173\t        Analyze acknowledgment speed signals.\n174\t\n175\t        Slow acks may indicate:\n176\t        - Poor narration quality (for audio)\n177\t        - Unclear result formatting\n178\t        - Wrong delivery surface\n179\t        \"\"\"\n180\t        proposals = []\n181\t\n182\t        # Calculate average ack delay\n183\t        delays = []\n184\t        for signal in signals:\n185\t            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n186\t            delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n187\t            delays.append(delay)\n188\t\n189\t        if not delays:\n190\t            return proposals\n191\t\n192\t        avg_delay = sum(delays) / len(delays)\n193\t\n194\t        # If average ack delay is high, propose improvements\n195\t        if avg_delay > 60:  # More than 1 minute average\n196\t            # Check which surface type has the slowest acks\n197\t            surface_delays = {}\n198\t            for signal in signals:\n199\t                data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n200\t                surface = data.get(\"surface_type\", \"unknown\")\n201\t                delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n202\t                if surface not in surface_delays:\n203\t                    surface_delays[surface] = []\n204\t                surface_delays[surface].append(delay)\n205\t\n206\t            # Find worst surface\n207\t            worst_surface = None\n208\t            worst_avg = 0\n209\t            for surface, s_delays in surface_delays.items():\n210\t                s_avg = sum(s_delays) / len(s_delays)\n211\t                if s_avg > worst_avg:\n212\t                    worst_avg = s_avg\n213\t                    worst_surface = surface\n214\t\n215\t            if worst_surface == \"audio\":\n216\t                # Propose improving narration prompts\n217\t                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n218\t                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n219\t                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n220\t                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n221\t                    artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n222\t                    change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s (target: <30s). Consider shorter, more concise summaries.\",\n223\t                    confidence=0.7,\n224\t                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n225\t                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n226\t                    session_ids=session_ids,\n227\t                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n228\t                ))\n229\t            elif worst_surface == \"canvas\":\n230\t                # Propose improving result formatting\n231\t                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n232\t                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n233\t                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n234\t                    artifact_type=\"component\",\n235\t                    artifact_name=\"result_card\",\n236\t                    change_summary=f\"Improve result card formatting: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s. Consider more prominent summaries and clearer action buttons.\",\n237\t                    confidence=0.65,\n238\t                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n239\t                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n240\t                    session_ids=session_ids,\n241\t                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n242\t                ))\n243\t\n244\t        return proposals\n245\t\n246\t    async def _analyze_follow_up_patterns(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> \"\"\"\n252\t        proposals = []\n253\t\n254\t        # Count follow-up types\n255\t        follow_up_types = {}\n256\t        for signal in signals:\n257\t            data = follow_up_types[f_type] = 0\n261\t            follow_up_types[f_type] += 1\n262\t\n263\t        # Generate proposals for common follow-up types\n264\t        for f_type, count in analysis processor for implicit feedback signals.\n\nReads feedback signals from the session store and proposes artifact updates\nas canvas cards for the self-modification agent to process.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..agents.self_modification import SelfModificationAgent, get_self_modification_agent\nfrom ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass AnalysisTrigger(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Triggers for background analysis.\"\"\"\n    SIGNAL_COUNT_THRESHOLD = \"signal_count_threshold\"  # Process after N signals\n    TIME_INTERVAL = \"time_interval\"  # Process every N seconds\n    HIGH_URGENCY_SIGNAL = \"high_urgency_signal\"  # Process immediately for critical signals\n    MANUAL = \"manual\"  # Triggered by user command\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AnalysisProposal:\n    \"\"\"A proposal for artifact update based on signal analysis.\"\"\"\n    proposal_id: str\n    signal_type: str\n    artifact_type: str  # 'prompt', 'config', 'component'\n    artifact_name: str\n    change_summary: str\n    confidence: float\n    signals_consulted: int\n    generated_at: int\n    session_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)\n    data: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_canvas_card(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to canvas card format for surfacing to user.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"id\": self.proposal_id,\n            \"type\": \"artifact_update_proposal\",\n            \"title\": f\"Update {self.artifact_type}: {self.artifact_name}\",\n            \"summary\": self.change_summary,\n            \"urgency\": \"normal\" if self.confidence < 0.8 else \"high\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"artifact_type\": self.artifact_type,\n                \"artifact_name\": self.artifact_name,\n                \"change_summary\": self.change_summary,\n                \"confidence\": self.confidence,\n                \"signals_consulted\": self.signals_consulted,\n            },\n            \"actions\": [\n                {\"id\": \"approve\", \"label\": \"Approve\", \"type\": \"primary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"reject\", \"label\": \"Reject\", \"type\": \"secondary\"},\n                {\"id\": \"defer\", \"label\": \"Defer\", \"type\": \"tertiary\"},\n            ],\n            \"created_at\": self.generated_at,\n        }\n\n\nclass BackgroundAnalysisProcessor:\n    \"\"\"\n    Processes implicit feedback signals and proposes artifact updates.\n\n    Runs as a background task that:\n    1. Reads unprocessed feedback signals from the session store\n    2. Analyzes patterns in the signals\n    3. Proposes artifact updates via self-modification agent\n    4. Surfaces proposals as canvas cards\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        signal_threshold: int = 10,\n        check_interval: int = 60,\n    ):\n        self.signal_threshold = signal_threshold\n        self.check_interval = check_interval\n        self.running = False\n        self.task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        self.store = get_store()\n        self.self_mod_agent = get_self_modification_agent()\n\n    async def analyze_signals(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze unprocessed feedback signals and generate proposals.\n\n        Args:\n            limit: Maximum number of signals to process in one batch\n\n        Returns:\n            List of proposals for artifact updates\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get unprocessed signals\n        signals = await self.store.get_unprocessed_signals(limit=limit)\n\n        if not signals:\n            logger.debug(\"No unprocessed signals to analyze\")\n            return []\n\n        logger.info(f\"Analyzing {len(signals)} feedback signals\")\n\n        # Group signals by type\n        signals_by_type = self._group_by_type(signals)\n\n        # Generate proposals for each signal type\n        proposals = []\n\n        for signal_type, type_signals in signals_by_type.items():\n            type_proposals = await self._analyze_signal_type(signal_type, type_signals)\n            proposals.extend(type_proposals)\n\n        # Mark processed signals\n        signal_ids = [s[\"signal_id\"] for s in signals]\n        await self.store.mark_signals_processed(signal_ids)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Generated {len(proposals)} proposals from {len(signals)} signals\")\n\n        return proposals\n\n    def _group_by_type(self, signals: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:\n        \"\"\"Group signals by type.\"\"\"\n        grouped = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            signal_type = signal[\"signal_type\"]\n            if signal_type not in grouped:\n                grouped[signal_type] = []\n            grouped[signal_type].append(signal)\n        return grouped\n\n    async def _analyze_signal_type(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str,\n        signals: list[dict],\n    ) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"Analyze signals of a specific type and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Extract unique session_ids from these signals\n        session_ids = set(s.get(\"session_id\") for s in signals if s.get(\"session_id\"))\n\n        if signal_type == \"ack_speed\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_ack_speed(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"follow_up_pattern\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_follow_up_patterns(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"surface_switch\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_surface_switches(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_requery\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_requeries(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"result_skipped\":\n            proposals.extend(await self._analyze_skipped_results(signals, session_ids))\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_continuation\":\n            # Topic continuation signals are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n        elif signal_type == \"topic_switch\":\n            # Topic switches are informational, don't generate proposals\n            pass\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_ack_speed(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze acknowledgment speed signals.\n\n        Slow acks may indicate:\n        - Poor narration quality (for audio)\n        - Unclear result formatting\n        - Wrong delivery surface\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Calculate average ack delay\n        delays = []\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n            delays.append(delay)\n\n        if not delays:\n            return proposals\n\n        avg_delay = sum(delays) / len(delays)\n\n        # If average ack delay is high, propose improvements\n        if avg_delay > 60:  # More than 1 minute average\n            # Check which surface type has the slowest acks\n            surface_delays = {}\n            for signal in signals:\n                data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n                surface = data.get(\"surface_type\", \"unknown\")\n                delay = data.get(\"ack_delay_seconds\", 0)\n                if surface not in surface_delays:\n                    surface_delays[surface] = []\n                surface_delays[surface].append(delay)\n\n            # Find worst surface\n            worst_surface = None\n            worst_avg = 0\n            for surface, s_delays in surface_delays.items():\n                s_avg = sum(s_delays) / len(s_delays)\n                if s_avg > worst_avg:\n                    worst_avg = s_avg\n                    worst_surface = surface\n\n            if worst_surface == \"audio\":\n                # Propose improving narration prompts\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s (target: <30s). Consider shorter, more concise summaries.\",\n                    confidence=0.7,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n            elif worst_surface == \"canvas\":\n                # Propose improving result formatting\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"ack_speed\",\n                    artifact_type=\"component\",\n                    artifact_name=\"result_card\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result card formatting: average ack delay is {avg_delay:.0f}s. Consider more prominent summaries and clearer action buttons.\",\n                    confidence=0.65,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"avg_delay_seconds\": avg_delay, \"worst_surface\": worst_surface},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_follow_up_patterns(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze follow-up pattern signals.\n\n        Follow-up patterns indicate what was missing from previous results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count follow-up types\n        follow_up_types = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            f_type = data.get(\"follow_up_type\", \"unknown\")\n            if f_type not in follow_up_types:\n                follow_up_types[f_type] = 0\n            follow_up_types[f_type] += 1\n\n        # Generate proposals for common follow-up types\n        for f_type, count in follow_up_types.items():\n            if count >= 3:  # Need at least 3 similar follow-ups to propose change\n                if f_type == \"why_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_explainatory\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add more 'why' explanations to results. {count} users asked 'why' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.8,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"temporal_question\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_timing\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Add timing information to results. {count} users asked 'how long/when' follow-up questions recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.75,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n                elif f_type == \"more_detail\":\n                    proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                        proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                        signal_type=\"follow_up_pattern\",\n                        artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                        artifact_name=\"synthesize_verbose\",\n                        change_summary=f\"Provide more detailed results by default. {count} users asked for more detail recently.\",\n                        confidence=0.7,\n                        signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                        generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                        session_ids=session_ids,\n                        data={\"follow_up_type\": f_type, \"count\": count},\n                    ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_surface_switches(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze surface switch signals.\n\n        Audio-to-canvas switches with pending results indicate poor narration.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count audio-to-canvas switches with pending results\n        audio_to_canvas_count = 0\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            if data.get(\"reason\") == \"audio_to_canvas_with_pending\":\n                audio_to_canvas_count += 1\n\n        if audio_to_canvas_count >= 5:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"surface_switch\",\n                artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                artifact_name=\"audio_narration\",\n                change_summary=f\"Improve audio narration quality. {audio_to_canvas_count} users switched to canvas with pending results, indicating narration was insufficient.\",\n                confidence=0.85,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                session_ids=session_ids,\n                data={\"audio_to_canvas_count\": audio_to_canvas_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_requeries(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze re-query signals.\n\n        Re-queries indicate inadequate results.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Group by utterance to find frequently re-queried items\n        requeries = {}\n        for signal in signals:\n            data = json.loads(signal.get(\"data\", \"{}\"))\n            utterance = data.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n            attempt_count = data.get(\"attempt_count\", 0)\n            if utterance and attempt_count > 1:\n                if utterance not in requeries:\n                    requeries[utterance] = 0\n                requeries[utterance] = max(requeries[utterance], attempt_count)\n\n        # Find queries with 3+ attempts\n        for utterance, max_attempts in requeries.items():\n            if max_attempts >= 3:\n                proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                    proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                    signal_type=\"result_requery\",\n                    artifact_type=\"prompt\",\n                    artifact_name=\"synthesize_completeness\",\n                    change_summary=f\"Improve result completeness for query type: '{utterance[:50]}...'. Users re-queried {max_attempts} times.\",\n                    confidence=0.75,\n                    signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                    generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                    session_ids=session_ids,\n                    data={\"utterance\": utterance, \"max_attempts\": max_attempts},\n                ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def _analyze_skipped_results(self, signals: list[dict], session_ids: set[str]) -> list[AnalysisProposal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Analyze skipped result signals.\n\n        Skipped results indicate low interest or poor delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        proposals = []\n\n        # Count skipped results\n        skipped_count = len(signals)\n\n        if skipped_count >= 10:\n            proposals.append(AnalysisProposal(\n                proposal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=\"result_skipped\",\n                artifact_type=\"config\",\n                artifact_name=\"monitoring\",\n                change_summary=f\"Reduce monitoring verbosity. {skipped_count} results were skipped (not acknowledged) before surface disconnect.\",\n                confidence=0.6,\n                signals_consulted=len(signals),\n                generated_at=int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n                session_ids=session_ids,\n                data={\"skipped_count\": skipped_count},\n            ))\n\n        return proposals\n\n    async def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main loop: periodically analyze signals and generate proposals.\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Starting background analysis processor (interval: {self.check_interval}s)\")\n\n        while self.running:\n            try:\n                # Analyze signals\n                proposals = await self.analyze_signals()\n\n                if proposals:\n                    # Surface proposals as canvas cards via SSE\n                    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n                    for proposal in proposals:\n                        card = proposal.to_canvas_card()\n\n                        # Broadcast to each relevant session\n                        for session_id in proposal.session_ids:\n                            event = SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"artifact_proposal\",\n                                data=card,\n                                target_session_id=session_id,\n                            )\n                            sent_count = await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n                            logger.info(\n                                f\"Broadcast proposal {proposal.proposal_id} to session {session_id}: \"\n                                f\"{proposal.change_summary} (sent to {sent_count} connections)\"\n                            )\n\n                # Wait for next cycle\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in background analysis loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.check_interval)\n\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n        if self.task is not None and not self.task.done():\n            logger.warning(\"Background analysis processor already running\")\n            return\n\n        self.running = True\n        self.task = asyncio.create_task(self.run())\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the background analysis processor.\"\"\"\n        self.running = False\n        if self.task:\n            self.task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self.task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Background analysis processor stopped\")\n\n\n# Global background analysis processor instance\n_background_processor: Optional[BackgroundAnalysisProcessor] = None\n\n\ndef get_background_processor(\n    signal_threshold: int = 10,\n    check_interval: int = 60,\n) -> BackgroundAnalysisProcessor:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global background analysis processor instance.\"\"\"\n    global _background_processor\n    if _background_processor is None:\n        _background_processor = BackgroundAnalysisProcessor(\n            signal_threshold=signal_threshold,\n            check_interval=check_interval,\n        )\n    return _background_processor\n","numLines":480,"startLine":1,"totalLines":480}}}

Clean up sentinel file\n        if SENTINEL_PATH.exists():\n            SENTINEL_PATH.unlink()\n\n    def test_unfrozen_by_default(self, clean_env):\n        \"\"\"Test that self-modification is unfrozen by default.\"\"\"\n        status = check_frozen()\n        assert not status.is_frozen\n        assert status.reason is None\n\n    def test_env_var_freeze(self, clean_env):\n        \"\"\"Test that ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1 freezes writes.\"\"\"\n        # cwd=cwd)\n149\t\n150\t\n151\tdef git_show(ref: str, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n152\t    \"\"\"\n153\t    Show git object content (e.g., 'HEAD:path/to/file').\n154\t\n155\t    Args:\n156\t        ref: Git reference (e.g., 'HEAD:path/to/file')\n157\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n158\t\n159\t    Returns:\n160\t        GitResult with show output\n161\t    \"\"\"\n162\t    return run_git_command(['show', ref], cwd=cwd)\n163\t\n164\t\n165\tdef git_rev_parse(ref: str, short: bool = False, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> GitResult:\n166\t    \"\"\"\n167\t    Get git SHA for a reference.\n168\t\n169\t    Args:\n170\t        ref: Git reference (e.g., 'HEAD')\n171\t        short: If True, return short SHA (default: False)\n172\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n173\t\n174\t    Returns:\n175\t        GitResult with SHA output\n176\t    \"\"\"\n177\t    args = ['rev-parse']\n178\t    if short:\n179\t        args.append('--short')\n180\t    args.append(ref)\n181\t    return run_git_command(args, cwd=cwd)\n182\t\n183\t\n184\tdef generate_self_mod_commit_message(file_path: Path, cwd: Optional[Path] = None) -> str:\n185\t    \"\"\"\n186\t    Generate a standardized commit message for self-modification writes.\n187\t\n188\t    Creates a commit message with the format:\n189\t    'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n190\t    where <commit-short-sha> is the short SHA of the current HEAD (the commit\n191\t    we are building on top of).\n192\t\n193\t    Args:\n194\t        file_path: Path to the file being modified (relative or absolute)\n195\t        cwd: Working directory (defaults to aide-de-camp repo root)\n196\t\n197\t    Returns:\n198\t        Commit message string with path and optional previous commit SHA\n199\t    \"\"\"\n200\t    if cwd is None:\n201\t        cwd = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n202\t\n203\t    # Get relative path from repo root\n204\t    try:\n205\t        rel_path = Path(file_path).relative_to(cwd)\n206\t    except ValueError:\n207\t        # If file_path is already relative or outside repo, use as-is\n208\t        rel_path = Path(file_path)\n209\t\n210\t    # Get the short SHA of the current HEAD (previous commit)\n211\t    # This will be included in the commit message to show what we're building on\n212\t    head_result = git_rev_parse('HEAD', short=True, cwd=cwd)\n213\t\n214\t    if head_result.success and head_result.stdout.strip():\n215\t        prev_commit_sha = head_result.stdout.strip()\n216\t        return f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path} [{prev_commit_sha}]\"\n217\t    else:\n218\t        # No previous commit (e.g., initial commit or empty repo)\n219\t        return f\"auto: self-mod write to {rel_path}\"\n220\t\n221\t# Prompt paths read per-invocation so edits take effect without a server restart\n222\t# (hot-reload), matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py and\n223\t# src/intent/router.py.\n224\tSELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_parse.md\")\n225\tSELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/self_mod_generate.md\")\n226\t\n227\t# Fallbacks used only if a prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n228\t_PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n229\t    \"You classify a user instruction to the artifact it targets. \"\n230\t    'Return ONLY JSON: {\"artifact_type\": \"prompt|config|component\", '\n231\t    '\"artifact_name\": \"<name>\", \"reasoning\": \"...\"}.'\n232\t)\n233\t_GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n234\t    \"You apply a user instruction to an artifact. Return ONLY JSON: \"\n235\t    '{\"updated_content\": \"<full updated text>\", \"change_summary\": \"one sentence\"}.'\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t\n239\tclass ArtifactType(Enum):\n240\t    \"\"\"Types of artifacts that can be modified.\"\"\"\n241\t    PROMPT = \"prompt\"\n242\t    CONFIG = \"config\"\n243\t    COMPONENT = \"component\"\n244\t\n245\t\n246\t@dataclass\n247\tclass ArtifactDiff:\n248\t    \"\"\"A diff showing changes to an artifact.\"\"\"\n249\t    artifact_name: str\n250\t    artifact_type: ArtifactType\n251\t    before: str\n252\t    after: str\n253\t    change_summary: str\n254\t    confidence: float\n255\t\n256\t\n257\t@dataclass\n258\tclass ModificationRequest:\n259\t    \"\"\"A user request to modify system behavior.\"\"\"\n260\t    instruction: str\n261\t    artifact_name: Optional[str]\n262\t    artifact_type: Optional[ArtifactType]\n263\t    context: Dict[str, Any]\n264\t\n265\t\n266\tclass SelfModificationAgent:\n267\t    \"\"\"\n268\t    Agent that modifies system artifacts based on user feedback.\n269\t\n270\t    Workflow:\n271\t    1. Receive user instruction\n272\t    2. Identify target artifact\n273\t    3. Read current artifact content\n274\t    4. Generate update\n275\t    5. Surface diff to user\n276\t    6. On approval: write artifact\n277\t    7. On rejection: discard\n278\t    \"\"\"\n279\t\n280\t    def __init__(\n281\t        self,\n282\t        parse_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n283\t        generate_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n284\t    ):\n285\t        self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n286\t        self.component_library = get_library()\n287\t        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n288\t        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_PARSE_PROMPT_PATH\n289\t        self.generate_prompt_path = generate_prompt_path or SELF_MOD_GENERATE_PROMPT_PATH\n290\t        self._zai_client = None\n291\t\n292\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n293\t        \"\"\"Get or create the ZAI proxy client (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n294\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n295\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n296\t        return self._zai_client\n297\t\n298\t    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n299\t        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n300\t        try:\n301\t            return path.read_text()\n302\t        except Exception as e:\n303\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt {path}: {e}\")\n304\t            return fallback\n305\t\n306\t    def _available_artifacts(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n307\t        \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n308\t        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n309\t        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n310\t            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n311\t            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n312\t            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n313\t        return artifacts\n314\t\n315\t    @staticmethod\n316\t    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n317\t        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n318\t        raw = raw.strip()\n319\t        if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n320\t            raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n321\t            raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n322\t        return raw\n323\t\n324\t    async def process_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ArtifactDiff:\n325\t        \"\"\"\n326\t        Process a user instruction for system modification.\n327\t\n328\t        Args:\n329\t            instruction: Natural language instruction\n330\t\n331\t        Returns:\n332\t            The proposed diff for user approval\n333\t        \"\"\"\n334\t        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n335\t        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n336\t\n337\t        # Get current content\n338\t        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n339\t\n340\t        # Generate update (LLM call)\n341\t        updated_content, change_summary = await self._generate_update(\n342\t            request,\n343\t            current_content\n344\t        )\n345\t\n346\t        diff = ArtifactDiff(\n347\t            artifact_name=request.artifact_name or \"unknown\",\n348\t            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n349\t            before=current_content,\n350\t            after=updated_content,\n351\t            change_summary=change_summary,\n352\t            confidence=self._estimate_confidence(request, change_summary)\n353\t        )\n354\t\n355\t        self._pending_diffs.append(diff)\n356\t        return diff\n357\t\n358\t    async def _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n359\t        \"\"\"\n360\t        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n361\t\n362\t        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n363\t        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n364\t        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n365\t        \"\"\"\n366\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n367\t        user_message = (\n368\t            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n369\t            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n370\t            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n371\t        )\n372\t\n373\t        try:\n374\t            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n375\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n376\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n377\t                user_message=user_message,\n378\t                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n379\t                max_tokens=512,\n380\t                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n381\t            )\n382\t            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n383\t\n384\t            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n385\t            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n386\t\n387\t            try:\n388\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n389\t            except ValueError:\n390\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n391\t\n392\t            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n393\t            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n394\t            # non-existent artifact.\n395\t            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n396\t            registered_set = set(registered)\n397\t            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n398\t                not name or name not in registered_set\n399\t            ):\n400\t                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n401\t                    name = \"router\"\n402\t                elif registered:\n403\t                    name = registered[0]\n404\t                else:\n405\t                    name = \"unknown\"\n406\t                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n407\t\n408\t            return ModificationRequest(\n409\t                instruction=instruction,\n410\t                artifact_name=name,\n411\t                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n412\t                context={\n413\t                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n414\t                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n415\t                },\n416\t            )\n417\t        except Exception as e:\n418\t            logger.warning(\n419\t                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n420\t            )\n421\t            return ModificationRequest(\n422\t                instruction=instruction,\n423\t                artifact_name=\"router\",\n424\t                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n425\t                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n426\t            )\n427\t\n428\t    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n429\t        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n430\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n431\t            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n432\t                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n433\t        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n434\t            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n435\t                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n436\t                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n437\t                if artifact:\n438\t                    return artifact.content\n439\t\n440\t        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n441\t\n442\t    async def _generate_update(\n443\t        self,\n444\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n445\t        current_content: str\n446\t    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n447\t        \"\"\"\n448\t        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n449\t\n450\t        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n451\t        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n452\t        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n453\t        \"\"\"\n454\t        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n455\t            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n456\t        )\n457\t        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n458\t        user_message = (\n459\t            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n460\t            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n461\t            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n462\t        )\n463\t\n464\t        try:\n465\t            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n466\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n467\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n468\t                user_message=user_message,\n469\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n470\t                max_tokens=4096,\n471\t                temperature=0.2,\n472\t            )\n473\t            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n474\t\n475\t            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n476\t            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n477\t            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n478\t                logger.warning(\n479\t                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n480\t                )\n481\t                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n482\t            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n483\t        except Exception as e:\n484\t            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n485\t            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n486\t\n487\t    def _estimate_confidence(\n488\t        self,\n489\t        request: ModificationRequest,\n490\t        change_summary: str\n491\t    ) -> float:\n492\t        \"\"\"\n493\t        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n494\t\n495\t        Higher confidence for:\n496\t        - Clear, specific instructions\n497\t        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n498\t        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n499\t        \"\"\"\n500\t        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n501\t\n502\t        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n503\t\n504\t        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n505\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n506\t            confidence += 0.2\n507\t\n508\t        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n509\t        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n510\t            confidence -= 0.2\n511\t\n512\t        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n513\t        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n514\t            confidence -= 0.1\n515\t\n516\t        # Clamp to valid range\n517\t        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n518\t\n519\t    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n520\t        \"\"\"\n521\t        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n522\t\n523\t        Args:\n524\t            diff: The diff to apply\n525\t\n526\t        Returns:\n527\t            True if successful, False otherwise\n528\t        \"\"\"\n529\t        try:\n530\t            # Check freeze protection before writing\n531\t            ensure_unfrozen()\n532\t\n533\t            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n534\t                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n535\t            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n536\t                return self._write_config(diff)\n537\t            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n538\t                return self._write_component(diff)\n539\t            return False\n540\t        except RuntimeError as e:\n541\t            # Clear error for freeze protection\n542\t            print(f\"Cannot apply diff: {e}\")\n543\t            return False\n544\t        except Exception as e:\n545\t            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n546\t            return False\n547\t\n548\t    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n549\t        \"\"\"\n550\t        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n551\t\n552\t        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n553\t        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n554\t\n555\t        Args:\n556\t            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n557\t            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n558\t        \"\"\"\n559\t        try:\n560\t            # Get the repo root directory\n561\t            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n562\t\n563\t            # Get relative path from repo root\n564\t            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n565\t\n566\t            # Verify the file exists before trying to stage it\n567\t            if not artifact_path.exists():\n568\t                logger.error(f\"Cannot commit artifact write: file does not exist at {artifact_path}\")\n569\t                return\n570\t\n571\t            # Stage the file for commit\n572\t            # This works for new files, modified files, and files that were deleted then recreated\n573\t            add_result = subprocess.run(\n574\t                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n575\t                cwd=repo_root,\n576\t                capture_output=True,\n577\t                text=True,\n578\t                check=False,\n579\t                timeout=10\n580\t            )\n581\t\n582\t            if add_result.returncode != 0:\n583\t                logger.error(f\"git add failed: stderr={add_result.stderr}, stdout={add_result.stdout}, returncode={add_result.returncode}\")\n584\t                return\n585\t\n586\t            # Generate standardized commit message with previous commit SHA\n587\t            commit_msg = generate_self_mod_commit_message(rel_path, cwd=repo_root)\n588\t\n589\t            # Create the commit (file is now staged)\n590\t            # Only commit staged changes, ignoring other unstaged changes\n591\t            result = subprocess.run(\n592\t                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg],\n593\t                cwd=repo_root,\n594\t                capture_output=True,\n595\t                text=True,\n596\t                check=False,\n597\t                timeout=10\n598\t            )\n599\t\n600\t            # Only log if commit was successful\n601\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n602\t                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n603\t                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n604\t                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n605\t                    cwd=repo_root,\n606\t                    capture_output=True,\n607\t                    text=True,\n608\t                    check=True,\n609\t                    timeout=10\n610\t                )\n611\t\n612\t                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n613\t                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n614\t            else:\n615\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n616\t\n617\t        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n618\t            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n619\t        except Exception as e:\n620\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n621\t\n622\t    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n623\t        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n624\t        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n625\t        if not artifact:\n626\t            return False\n627\t\n628\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n629\t            f.write(diff.after)\n630\t\n631\t        # Force reload to pick up changes\n632\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n633\t\n634\t        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n635\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n636\t\n637\t        return True\n638\t\n639\t    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n640\t        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n641\t        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n642\t        if not artifact:\n643\t            return False\n644\t\n645\t        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n646\t            f.write(diff.after)\n647\t\n648\t        # Force reload\n649\t        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n650\t\n651\t        # Create git commit for the config write\n652\t        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n653\t\n654\t        return True\n655\t\n656\t    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n657\t        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n658\t        # For components, we need to identify the component\n659\t        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n660\t        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n661\t            return False\n662\t\n663\t        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n664\t        if not component:\n665\t            return False\n666\t\n667\t        self.component_library.update_component(\n668\t            component.id,\n669\t            diff.after,\n670\t            diff.change_summary\n671\t        )\n672\t        return True\n673\t\n674\t    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n675\t        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n676\t        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n677\t            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n678\t\n679\t    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n680\t        \"\"\"\n681\t        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n682\t\n683\t        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n684\t        For components: use component version history\n685\t        \"\"\"\n686\t        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n687\t            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n688\t\n689\t        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n690\t        import subprocess\n691\t        try:\n692\t            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n693\t            if not artifact:\n694\t                return False\n695\t\n696\t            # Get previous version from git\n697\t            result = subprocess.run(\n698\t                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n699\t                capture_output=True,\n700\t                text=True,\n701\t                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n702\t            )\n703\t\n704\t            if result.returncode == 0:\n705\t                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n706\t                    f.write(result.stdout)\n707\t                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n708\t                return True\n709\t        except Exception as e:\n710\t            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n711\t\n712\t        return False\n713\t\n714\t    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n715\t        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n716\t        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n717\t        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n718\t            return False\n719\t\n720\t        target_version = component.version - 1\n721\t        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n722\t        return True\n723\t\n724\t    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n725\t        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n726\t        return Receive user instruction\n    2. Identify target artifact\n    3. Read current artifact content\n    4. Generate update\n    5. Surface diff to user\n    6. On approval: write artifact\n    7. On rejection: discard\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        parse_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n        generate_prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n    ):\n        self.reload_mgr = get_reload_manager()\n        self.component_library = get_library()\n        self._pending_diffs: List[ArtifactDiff] = []\n        self.parse_prompt_path = parse_prompt_path or \"\"\"Get or create the ZAI proxy client (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    def _load_prompt(self, path: Path, fallback: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load a self-modification prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load prompt {path}: {e}\")\n            return fallback\n\n    def _available_artifacts(self) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Build the list of registered artifacts for the parser prompt.\"\"\"\n        artifacts: List[Dict[str, str]] = []\n        for name, path_str in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().items():\n            suffix = Path(path_str).suffix.lower()\n            type_str = \"config\" if suffix in (\".yaml\", \".yml\") else \"prompt\"\n            artifacts.append({\"name\": name, \"type\": type_str})\n        return artifacts\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _strip_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Strip ```json ... ``` markdown fences from a GLM response.\"\"\"\n        raw = raw.strip()\n        if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n            raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n            raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n        return raw\n\n    async def process_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ArtifactDiff:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a user instruction for system modification.\n\n        Args:\n            instruction: Natural language instruction\n\n        Returns:\n            The proposed diff for user approval\n        \"\"\"\n        # Parse the instruction to identify target (LLM call)\n        request = await self._parse_instruction(instruction)\n\n        # Get current content\n        current_content = self._get_artifact_content(request)\n\n        # Generate update (LLM call)\n        updated_content, \"unknown\",\n            artifact_type=request.artifact_type or ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n            before=current_content,\n            after=updated_content,\n            change_summary=change_summary,\n            confidence=self._estimate_confidence(request, change_summary)\n        )\n\n        self._pending_diffs.append(diff)\n        return diff\n\n    async def _parse_instruction(self, instruction: str) -> ModificationRequest:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse an instruction to identify the target artifact via an LLM call.\n\n        The LLM classifies the free-text instruction against the registered\n        artifacts and returns the artifact_type + artifact_name. Falls back to\n        the router prompt if the call or its response cannot be parsed.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(self.parse_prompt_path, _PARSE_PROMPT_FALLBACK)\n        user_message = (\n            \"## Registered Artifacts\\n\"\n            + json.dumps(self._available_artifacts(), indent=2)\n            + f\"\\n\\n## User Instruction\\n{instruction}\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.HAIKU.value,  # cheap, fast classification\n                max_tokens=512,\n                temperature=0.0,  # deterministic classification\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            type_str = data.get(\"artifact_type\", \"prompt\")\n            name = data.get(\"artifact_name\")\n\n            try:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType(type_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            # Validate the name is actually registered; fall back to a known\n            # artifact (preferring the router prompt) so we never target a\n            # non-existent artifact.\n            registered = list(self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts().keys())\n            registered_set = set(registered)\n            if artifact_type != ArtifactType.COMPONENT and (\n                not name or name not in registered_set\n            ):\n                if \"router\" in registered_set:\n                    name = \"router\"\n                elif registered:\n                    name = registered[0]\n                else:\n                    name = \"unknown\"\n                artifact_type = ArtifactType.PROMPT\n\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=name,\n                artifact_type=artifact_type,\n                context={\n                    \"raw_instruction\": instruction,\n                    \"reasoning\": data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                },\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"_parse_instruction LLM parse failed, defaulting to router prompt: {e}\"\n            )\n            return ModificationRequest(\n                instruction=instruction,\n                artifact_name=\"router\",\n                artifact_type=ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n                context={\"raw_instruction\": instruction, \"fallback\": True},\n            )\n\n    def _get_artifact_content(self, request: ModificationRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current content of the target artifact.\"\"\"\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                return self.reload_mgr.get_prompt(request.artifact_name)\n        elif request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            if request.artifact_name in self.reload_mgr.list_artifacts():\n                # For configs, return YAML as string for diff\n                artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(request.artifact_name)\n                if artifact:\n                    return artifact.content\n\n        return \"# Artifact not found or not loaded\"\n\n    async def _generate_update(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        current_content: str\n    ) -> Tuple[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate updated artifact content via an LLM call.\n\n        Sends the current content + instruction to the LLM and returns the full\n        updated content plus a change summary. On failure, returns the content\n        unchanged with an honest summary rather than fabricating a change.\n        \"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_prompt(\n            self.generate_prompt_path, _GENERATE_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n        )\n        artifact_type = request.artifact_type.value if request.artifact_type else \"prompt\"\n        user_message = (\n            f\"## Instruction\\n{request.instruction}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Artifact Type\\n{artifact_type}\\n\\n\"\n            f\"## Current Content\\n```\\n{current_content}\\n```\\n\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            client = await self._get_zai_client()\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,  # higher quality for rewriting\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.2,\n            )\n            data = json.loads(self._strip_fences(response))\n\n            updated = data.get(\"updated_content\")\n            summary = data.get(\"change_summary\", \"\")\n            if not isinstance(updated, str) or not updated.strip():\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"_generate_update returned no updated_content; leaving artifact unchanged\"\n                )\n                return current_content, summary or \"No update generated\"\n            return updated, summary or \"Updated artifact\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"_generate_update LLM call failed: {e}\")\n            return current_content, f\"Update generation failed: {e}\"\n\n    def _estimate_confidence(\n        self,\n        request: ModificationRequest,\n        change_summary: str\n    ) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Estimate confidence in the proposed change.\n\n        Higher confidence for:\n        - Clear, specific instructions\n        - Additive changes (vs destructive)\n        - Low-risk artifacts (prompts vs registry)\n        \"\"\"\n        confidence = 0.5  # Base confidence\n\n        instruction_lower = request.instruction.lower()\n\n        # Specific instructions increase confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"add\", \"include\", \"always\"]):\n            confidence += 0.2\n\n        # Destructive keywords decrease confidence\n        if any(word in instruction_lower for word in [\"remove\", \"delete\", \"change entirely\"]):\n            confidence -= 0.2\n\n        # Config changes are riskier than prompt changes\n        if request.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n            confidence -= 0.1\n\n        # Clamp to valid range\n        return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))\n\n    def apply_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply a diff by writing the updated artifact.\n\n        Args:\n            diff: The diff to apply\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successful, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Check freeze protection before writing\n            ensure_unfrozen()\n\n            if diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.PROMPT:\n                return self._write_prompt(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.CONFIG:\n                return self._write_config(diff)\n            elif diff.artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n                return self._write_component(diff)\n            return False\n        except RuntimeError as e:\n            # Clear error for freeze protection\n            print(f\"Cannot apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Failed to apply diff: {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _commit_artifact_write(self, artifact_path: Path, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a git commit for an artifact write.\n\n        Creates a commit with a machine-generated message following the convention:\n        'auto: self-mod write to <path> [<commit-short-sha>]'\n\n        Args:\n            artifact_path: Path to the artifact that was written\n            artifact_type: Type of artifact (prompt/config)\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get the repo root directory\n            repo_root = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\")\n\n            # Get relative path from repo root\n            rel_path = artifact_path.relative_to(repo_root)\n\n            # Verify the file exists before trying to stage it\n            if not artifact_path.exists():\n                logger.error(f\"Cannot commit artifact write: file does not exist at {artifact_path}\")\n                return\n\n            # Stage the file for commit\n            # This works for new files, modified files, and files that were deleted then recreated\n            add_result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'add', str(rel_path)],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            if add_result.returncode != 0:\n                logger.error(f\"git add failed: stderr={add_result.stderr}, stdout={add_result.stdout}, returncode={add_result.returncode}\")\n                return\n\n            # Generate standardized commit message with previous commit SHA\n            commit_msg = generate_self_mod_commit_message(rel_path, cwd=repo_root)\n\n            # Create the commit (file is now staged)\n            # Only commit staged changes, ignoring other unstaged changes\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'commit', '-m', commit_msg],\n                cwd=repo_root,\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                check=False,\n                timeout=10\n            )\n\n            # Only log if commit was successful\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                # Get the short SHA of the commit just created\n                sha_result = subprocess.run(\n                    ['git', 'rev-parse', '--short', 'HEAD'],\n                    cwd=repo_root,\n                    capture_output=True,\n                    text=True,\n                    check=True,\n                    timeout=10\n                )\n\n                short_sha = sha_result.stdout.strip()\n                logger.info(f\"Created git commit {short_sha} for {artifact_type.value} write to {rel_path}\")\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create git commit: stdout={result.stdout}, stderr={result.stderr}, returncode={result.returncode}\")\n\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            logger.error(\"Git command timed out\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create git commit for artifact write: {e}\")\n\n    def _write_prompt(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated prompt file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload to pick up changes\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the prompt write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_config(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated config file.\"\"\"\n        artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not artifact:\n            return False\n\n        with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n            f.write(diff.after)\n\n        # Force reload\n        self.reload_mgr.force_reload(diff.artifact_name)\n\n        # Create git commit for the config write\n        self._commit_artifact_write(artifact.path, diff.artifact_type)\n\n        return True\n\n    def _write_component(self, diff: ArtifactDiff) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Write updated component to library.\"\"\"\n        # For components, we need to identify the component\n        # This is a placeholder - in production, parse component_id from instruction\n        if not diff.artifact_name.startswith(\"comp-\"):\n            return False\n\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(diff.artifact_name)\n        if not component:\n            return False\n\n        self.component_library.update_component(\n            component.id,\n            diff.after,\n            diff.change_summary\n        )\n        return True\n\n    def reject_diff(self, diff: ArtifactDiff):\n        \"\"\"Discard a diff without applying it.\"\"\"\n        if diff in self._pending_diffs:\n            self._pending_diffs.remove(diff)\n\n    def rollback(self, artifact_name: str, artifact_type: ArtifactType) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\n\n        For prompts/configs: read from git history\n        For components: use component version history\n        \"\"\"\n        if artifact_type == ArtifactType.COMPONENT:\n            return self._rollback_component(artifact_name)\n\n        # For prompts/configs, use git to get previous version\n        import subprocess\n        try:\n            artifact = self.reload_mgr._artifacts.get(artifact_name)\n            if not artifact:\n                return False\n\n            # Get previous version from git\n            result = subprocess.run(\n                ['git', 'show', f'HEAD:{artifact.path.name}'],\n                capture_output=True,\n                text=True,\n                cwd=artifact.path.parent\n            )\n\n            if result.returncode == 0:\n                with open(artifact.path, 'w') as f:\n                    f.write(result.stdout)\n                self.reload_mgr.force_reload(artifact_name)\n                return True\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"Rollback failed: {e}\")\n\n        return False\n\n    def _rollback_component(self, component_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Rollback a component using its version history.\"\"\"\n        component = self.component_library.get_component(component_id)\n        if not component or component.version <= 1:\n            return False\n\n        target_version = component.version - 1\n        self.component_library.rollback_component(component_id, target_version)\n        return True\n\n    def list_pending_diffs(self) -> List[ArtifactDiff]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending diffs awaiting approval.\"\"\"\n        return self._pending_diffs.copy()\n\n    def clear_pending_diffs(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all pending diffs.\"\"\"\n        self._pending_diffs.clear()\n\n\n# Singleton instance\n_agent: Optional[SelfModificationAgent] = None\n\n\ndef get_self_modification_agent() -> SelfModificationAgent:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the self-modification agent singleton.\"\"\"\n    global _agent\n    if _agent is None:\n        _agent = SelfModificationAgent()\n    return _agent\n","numLines":743,"startLine":1,"totalLines":743}}}
