How quickly user acknowledged a result\n26\t    FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN = \"follow_up_pattern\"  # Follow-up question patterns\n27\t    SURFACE_SWITCH = \"surface_switch\"  # Audio-to-canvas switches\n28\t    RESULT_REQUERY = \"result_requery\"  # User asked same question again\n29\t    RESULT_SKIPPED = \"result_skipped\"  # User didn't acknowledge result\n30\t    TOPIC_CONTINUATION = \"topic_continuation\"  # User continued same topic\n31\t    TOPIC_SWITCH = \"topic_switch\"  # User switched topics\n32\t\n33\t\n34\t@dataclass\n35\tclass FeedbackSignal:\n36\t    \"\"\"A single implicit feedback signal.\"\"\"\n37\t    signal_id: str\n38\t    signal_type: SignalType\n39\t    session_id: str\n40\t    result_id: Optional[str]\n41\t    topic_id: Optional[str]\n42\t    timestamp: int\n43\t    data: dict[str, Any]\n44\t    surface_type: Optional[str] = None\n45\t\n46\t\n47\tclass ImplicitFeedbackTracker:\n48\t    \"\"\"\n49\t    Tracks implicit user feedback signals.\n50\t\n51\t    Monitors:\n52\t    - Result acknowledgment speed (fast = positive, slow = negative)\n53\t    - Follow-up question patterns (indicates what was missing)\n54\t    - Surface switch timing (audio-to-canvas indicates poor narration)\n55\t    - Result re-queries (indicates inadequate result)\n56\t    - Topic continuation vs switch\n57\t    \"\"\"\n58\t\n59\t    def __init__(self):\n60\t        # Track result creation times for ack speed calculation\n61\t        self._result_created_at: dict[str, int] = {}  # result_id -> created_at\n62\t        # Track recent utterances per topic for pattern detection\n63\t        self._recent_utterances: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}  # session_id -> [{utterance, topic_id, timestamp}]\n64\t        # Track re-query attempts\n65\t        self._query_attempts: dict[str, list[str]] = {}  # normalized_query -> [result_ids]\n66\t        # Track surface switches\n67\t        self._surface_switches: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}  # session_id -> [{from_surface, to_surface, timestamp, result_id}]\n68\t\n69\t    async def track_result_created(self, result_id: str, session_id: str, topic_id: Optional[str]) -> None:\n70\t        \"\"\"Track when a result was created for ack speed calculation.\"\"\"\n71\t        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n72\t        self._result_created_at[result_id] = now\n73\t        logger.debug(f\"Tracked result creation: {result_id} at {now}\")\n74\t\n75\t    async def track_result_acknowledged(\n76\t        self,\n77\t        result_id: str,\n78\t        session_id: str,\n79\t        surface_type: str,\n80\t    ) -> Optional[FeedbackSignal]:\n81\t        \"\"\"\n82\t        Track when a user acknowledged a result.\n83\t\n84\t        Generates an ack_speed signal.\n85\t        \"\"\"\n86\t        if result_id not in self._result_created_at:\n87\t            logger.warning(f\"Result {result_id} not tracked for creation time\")\n88\t            return None\n89\t\n90\t        created_at = self._result_created_at[result_id]\n91\t        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n92\t        ack_delay_seconds = now - created_at\n93\t\n94\t        # Clean up old entries\n95\t        del self._result_created_at[result_id]\n96\t\n97\t        # Determine signal quality\n98\t        # Fast ack (< 10s) = positive signal\n99\t        # Medium ack (10-60s) = neutral\n100\t        # Slow ack (> 60s) = negative signal\n101\t        # Very slow ack (> 300s) = strongly negative (may have been ignored)\n102\t        if ack_delay_seconds < 10:\n103\t            quality = \"positive\"\n104\t        elif ack_delay_seconds < 60:\n105\t            quality = \"neutral\"\n106\t        elif ack_delay_seconds < 300:\n107\t            quality = \"negative\"\n108\t        else:\n109\t            quality = \"strongly_negative\"\n110\t\n111\t        signal = FeedbackSignal(\n112\t            signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n113\t            signal_type=SignalType.ACK_SPEED,\n114\t            session_id=session_id,\n115\t            result_id=result_id,\n116\t            topic_id=None,  # We don't track topic for ack speed\n117\t            timestamp=now,\n118\t            data={\n119\t                \"ack_delay_seconds\": ack_delay_seconds,\n120\t                \"quality\": quality,\n121\t                \"surface_type\": surface_type,\n122\t            },\n123\t            surface_type=surface_type,\n124\t        )\n125\t\n126\t        await self._store_signal(signal)\n127\t        logger.info(f\"Tracked ack_speed signal: {ack_delay_seconds}s ({quality})\")\n128\t\n129\t        return signal\n130\t\n131\t    async def track_utterance(\n132\t        self,\n133\t        session_id: str,\n134\t        utterance: str,\n135\t        topic_id: Optional[str],\n136\t        is_follow_up: bool,\n137\t    ) -> None:\n138\t        \"\"\"\n139\t        Track an utterance for pattern detection.\n140\t\n141\t        Generates follow_up_pattern and topic_switch/continuation signals.\n142\t        \"\"\"\n143\t        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n144\t\n145\t        # Store utterance\n146\t        if session_id not in self._recent_utterances:\n147\t            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = []\n148\t\n149\t        self._recent_utterances[session_id].append({\n150\t            \"utterance\": utterance,\n151\t            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n152\t            \"timestamp\": now,\n153\t            \"is_follow_up\": is_follow_up,\n154\t        })\n155\t\n156\t        # Keep only last 50 utterances\n157\t        if len(self._recent_utterances[session_id]) > 50:\n158\t            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = self._recent_utterances[session_id][-50:]\n159\t\n160\t        # Check for follow-up pattern\n161\t        if is_follow_up and topic_id:\n162\t            await self._analyze_follow_up_pattern(session_id, utterance, topic_id, now)\n163\t\n164\t        # Check for topic switch vs continuation\n165\t        await self._analyze_topic_switch(session_id, topic_id, now)\n166\t\n167\t    async def _analyze_follow_up_pattern(\n168\t        self,\n169\t        session_id: str,\n170\t        utterance: str,\n171\t        topic_id: str,\n172\t        timestamp: int,\n173\t    ) -> None:\n174\t        \"\"\"Analyze follow-up patterns to detect what was missing from previous result.\"\"\"\n175\t        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n176\t\n177\t        # Look for the previous utterance on the same topic\n178\t        prev_utterance = None\n179\t        for u in reversed(utterances[:-1]):  # Exclude current utterance\n180\t            if u[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id:\n181\t                prev_utterance = u\n182\t                break\n183\t\n184\t        if not prev_utterance:\n185\t            return\n186\t\n187\t        # Analyze follow-up type\n188\t        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n189\t\n190\t        follow_up_type = None\n191\t        if any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"why\", \"how come\", \"reason\"]):\n192\t            follow_up_type = \"why_question\"  # Previous result lacked explanation\n193\t        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"how long\", \"when\", \"time\"]):\n194\t            follow_up_type = \"temporal_question\"  # Previous result lacked timing info\n195\t        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"what else\", \"more detail\", \"tell me more\"]):\n196\t            follow_up_type = \"more_detail\"  # Previous result was insufficient\n197\t        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"status\", \"current state\", \"what's happening\"]):\n198\t            follow_up_type = \"status_check\"  # User checking if state changed\n199\t        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"fix\", \"solve\", \"resolve\"]):\n200\t            follow_up_type = \"action_request\"  # User wants to take action\n201\t\n202\t        if follow_up_type:\n203\t            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n204\t                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n205\t                signal_type=SignalType.FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN,\n206\t                session_id=session_id,\n207\t                result_id=None,\n208\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n209\t                timestamp=timestamp,\n210\t                data={\n211\t                    \"follow_up_type\": follow_up_type,\n212\t                    \"utterance\": utterance,\n213\t                    \"prev_utterance\": prev_utterance[\"utterance\"],\n214\t                    \"time_since_prev\": timestamp - prev_utterance[\"timestamp\"],\n215\t                },\n216\t            )\n217\t\n218\t            await self._store_signal(signal)\n219\t            logger.info(f\"Tracked follow_up_pattern: {follow_up_type}\")\n220\t\n221\t    async def _analyze_topic_switch(\n222\t        self,\n223\t        session_id: str,\n224\t        topic_id: Optional[str],\n225\t        timestamp: int,\n226\t    ) -> None:\n227\t        \"\"\"Analyze whether user switched topics or continued.\"\"\"\n228\t        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n229\t\n230\t        if len(utterances) < 2:\n231\t            return\n232\t\n233\t        # Feedback Signal Collection for aide-de-camp.\n\nTracks user behavior and generates implicit feedback signals\nfor the background analysis bead to process.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass SignalType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Types of implicit feedback signals.\"\"\"\n    ACK_SPEED = \"ack_speed\"  # How quickly user acknowledged a result\n    FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN = \"follow_up_pattern\"  # Follow-up question patterns\n    SURFACE_SWITCH = \"surface_switch\"  # Audio-to-canvas switches\n    RESULT_REQUERY = \"result_requery\"  # User asked same question again\n    RESULT_SKIPPED = \"result_skipped\"  # User didn't acknowledge result\n    TOPIC_CONTINUATION = \"topic_continuation\"  # User continued same topic\n    TOPIC_SWITCH = \"topic_switch\"  # User switched topics\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass FeedbackSignal:\n    \"\"\"A single implicit feedback signal.\"\"\"\n    signal_id: str\n    signal_type: SignalType\n    session_id: str\n    result_id: Optional[str]\n    topic_id: Optional[str]\n    timestamp: int\n    data: dict[str, Any]\n    surface_type: Optional[str] = None\n\n\nclass ImplicitFeedbackTracker:\n    \"\"\"\n    Tracks implicit user feedback signals.\n\n    Monitors:\n    - Result acknowledgment speed (fast = positive, slow = negative)\n    - Follow-up question patterns (indicates what was missing)\n    - Surface switch timing (audio-to-canvas indicates poor narration)\n    - Result re-queries (indicates inadequate result)\n    - Topic continuation vs switch\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Track result creation times for ack speed calculation\n        self._result_created_at: dict[str, int] = {}  # result_id -> created_at\n        # Track recent utterances per topic for pattern detection\n        self._recent_utterances: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}  # session_id -> [{utterance, topic_id, timestamp}]\n        # Track re-query attempts\n        self._query_attempts: dict[str, list[str]] = {}  # normalized_query -> [result_ids]\n        # Track surface switches\n        self._surface_switches: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}  # session_id -> [{from_surface, to_surface, timestamp, result_id}]\n\n    async def track_result_created(self, result_id: str, session_id: str, topic_id: Optional[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Track when a result was created for ack speed calculation.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        self._result_created_at[result_id] = now\n        logger.debug(f\"Tracked result creation: {result_id} at {now}\")\n\n    async def track_result_acknowledged(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> Optional[FeedbackSignal]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track when a user acknowledged a result.\n\n        Generates an ack_speed signal.\n        \"\"\"\n        if result_id not in self._result_created_at:\n            logger.warning(f\"Result {result_id} not tracked for creation time\")\n            return None\n\n        created_at = self._result_created_at[result_id]\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n        ack_delay_seconds = now - created_at\n\n        # Clean up old entries\n        del self._result_created_at[result_id]\n\n        # Determine signal quality\n        # Fast ack (< 10s) = positive signal\n        # Medium ack (10-60s) = neutral\n        # Slow ack (> 60s) = negative signal\n        # Very slow ack (> 300s) = strongly negative (may have been ignored)\n        if ack_delay_seconds < 10:\n            quality = \"positive\"\n        elif ack_delay_seconds < 60:\n            quality = \"neutral\"\n        elif ack_delay_seconds < 300:\n            quality = \"negative\"\n        else:\n            quality = \"strongly_negative\"\n\n        signal = FeedbackSignal(\n            signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n            signal_type=SignalType.ACK_SPEED,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            topic_id=None,  # We don't track topic for ack speed\n            timestamp=now,\n            data={\n                \"ack_delay_seconds\": ack_delay_seconds,\n                \"quality\": quality,\n                \"surface_type\": surface_type,\n            },\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n\n        await self._store_signal(signal)\n        logger.info(f\"Tracked ack_speed signal: {ack_delay_seconds}s ({quality})\")\n\n        return signal\n\n    async def track_utterance(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: Optional[str],\n        is_follow_up: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track an utterance for pattern detection.\n\n        Generates follow_up_pattern and topic_switch/continuation signals.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        # Store utterance\n        if session_id not in self._recent_utterances:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = []\n\n        self._recent_utterances[session_id].append({\n            \"utterance\": utterance,\n            \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n            \"timestamp\": now,\n            \"is_follow_up\": is_follow_up,\n        })\n\n        # Keep only last 50 utterances\n        if len(self._recent_utterances[session_id]) > 50:\n            self._recent_utterances[session_id] = self._recent_utterances[session_id][-50:]\n\n        # Check for follow-up pattern\n        if is_follow_up and topic_id:\n            await self._analyze_follow_up_pattern(session_id, utterance, topic_id, now)\n\n        # Check for topic switch vs continuation\n        await self._analyze_topic_switch(session_id, topic_id, now)\n\n    async def _analyze_follow_up_pattern(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        timestamp: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Analyze follow-up patterns to detect what was missing from previous result.\"\"\"\n        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n\n        # Look for the previous utterance on the same topic\n        prev_utterance = None\n        for u in reversed(utterances[:-1]):  # Exclude current utterance\n            if u[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id:\n                prev_utterance = u\n                break\n\n        if not prev_utterance:\n            return\n\n        # Analyze follow-up type\n        utterance_lower = utterance.lower()\n\n        follow_up_type = None\n        if any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"why\", \"how come\", \"reason\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"why_question\"  # Previous result lacked explanation\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"how long\", \"when\", \"time\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"temporal_question\"  # Previous result lacked timing info\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"what else\", \"more detail\", \"tell me more\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"more_detail\"  # Previous result was insufficient\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"status\", \"current state\", \"what's happening\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"status_check\"  # User checking if state changed\n        elif any(word in utterance_lower for word in [\"fix\", \"solve\", \"resolve\"]):\n            follow_up_type = \"action_request\"  # User wants to take action\n\n        if follow_up_type:\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.FOLLOW_UP_PATTERN,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"follow_up_type\": follow_up_type,\n                    \"utterance\": utterance,\n                    \"prev_utterance\": prev_utterance[\"utterance\"],\n                    \"time_since_prev\": timestamp - prev_utterance[\"timestamp\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked follow_up_pattern: {follow_up_type}\")\n\n    async def _analyze_topic_switch(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_id: Optional[str],\n        timestamp: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Analyze whether user switched topics or continued.\"\"\"\n        utterances = self._recent_utterances.get(session_id, [])\n\n        if len(utterances) < 2:\n            return\n\n        # Compare with previous utterance\n        prev_utterance = utterances[-2]\n        prev_topic_id = prev_utterance.get(\"topic_id\")\n\n        if prev_topic_id == topic_id and topic_id is not None:\n            # Topic continuation\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.TOPIC_CONTINUATION,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"turn_count\": sum(1 for u in utterances if u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id),\n                    \"time_in_topic\": timestamp - min(\n                        u[\"timestamp\"] for u in utterances if u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id\n                    ) if any(u.get(\"topic_id\") == topic_id for u in utterances) else 0,\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n\n        elif prev_topic_id != topic_id and prev_topic_id and topic_id:\n            # Topic switch\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.TOPIC_SWITCH,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                timestamp=timestamp,\n                data={\n                    \"from_topic_id\": prev_topic_id,\n                    \"to_topic_id\": topic_id,\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked topic_switch: {prev_topic_id} -> {topic_id}\")\n\n    async def track_surface_switch(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        from_surface: str,\n        to_surface: str,\n        pending_result_count: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track a surface switch event.\n\n        Generates surface_switch signal. Audio-to-canvas with pending results\n        indicates poor narration or complex data.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        if session_id not in self._surface_switches:\n            self._surface_switches[session_id] = []\n\n        switch_data = {\n            \"from_surface\": from_surface,\n            \"to_surface\": to_surface,\n            \"timestamp\": now,\n            \"pending_result_count\": pending_result_count,\n        }\n\n        self._surface_switches[session_id].append(switch_data)\n\n        # Keep only last 20 switches\n        if len(self._surface_switches[session_id]) > 20:\n            self._surface_switches[session_id] = self._surface_switches[session_id][-20:]\n\n        # If switching from audio to canvas with pending results, signal\n        if from_surface == \"audio\" and to_surface == \"canvas\" and pending_result_count > 0:\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.SURFACE_SWITCH,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=None,\n                topic_id=None,\n                timestamp=now,\n                data={\n                    \"from_surface\": from_surface,\n                    \"to_surface\": to_surface,\n                    \"pending_result_count\": pending_result_count,\n                    \"reason\": \"audio_to_canvas_with_pending\",\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked surface_switch: audio->canvas with {pending_result_count} pending results\")\n\n    async def track_requery(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        utterance: str,\n        previous_result_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track a re-query (user asked same/similar question again).\n\n        Indicates previous result was inadequate.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        # Normalize utterance for comparison\n        normalized = self._normalize_utterance(utterance)\n\n        if normalized not in self._query_attempts:\n            self._query_attempts[normalized] = []\n\n        self._query_attempts[normalized].extend(previous_result_ids)\n\n        # If we've seen this query before, signal re-query\n        if len(self._query_attempts[normalized]) > len(previous_result_ids):\n            signal = FeedbackSignal(\n                signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n                signal_type=SignalType.RESULT_REQUERY,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                result_id=previous_result_ids[-1] if previous_result_ids else None,\n                topic_id=None,\n                timestamp=now,\n                data={\n                    \"utterance\": utterance,\n                    \"normalized\": normalized,\n                    \"previous_result_ids\": previous_result_ids,\n                    \"attempt_count\": len(self._query_attempts[normalized]),\n                },\n            )\n\n            await self._store_signal(signal)\n            logger.info(f\"Tracked result_requery: attempt {len(self._query_attempts[normalized])}\")\n\n    async def track_result_skipped(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        time_until_skip: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Track when a result was skipped (not acknowledged before surface disconnect).\n\n        Indicates low interest or poor delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp())\n\n        signal = FeedbackSignal(\n            signal_id=str(uuid4()),\n            signal_type=SignalType.RESULT_SKIPPED,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            result_id=result_id,\n            topic_id=None,\n            timestamp=now,\n            data={\n                \"time_until_skip_seconds\": _store_signal(self, signal: FeedbackSignal) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Store a signal in the session store.\"\"\"\n        store = get_store()\n        await store.create_feedback_signal(\n            signal_id=signal.signal_id,\n            signal_type=signal.signal_type.value,\n            session_id=signal.session_id,\n            result_id=signal.result_id,\n            topic_id=signal.topic_id,\n            timestamp=signal.timestamp,\n            data=signal.data,\n        )\n\n    def _normalize_utterance(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize       limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get recent signals for a session (for background analysis).\"\"\"\n        # This would query the session store in production\n        # self._recent_utterances.pop(session_id, None)\n        self._surface_switches.pop(session_id, None)\n        logger.debug(f\"Cleared feedback tracking for session {session_id}\")\n\n\n# Global {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_bc0cf76906e5441b96edd70b","type":"tool_result","content":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:- Out-of-band kill switch: self-modification can break the very interface used to undo it (a bad router prompt means no more routed instructions). Two escape hatches bypass the interface entirely: `ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1` in the server environment, or `adc freeze` from the CLI (which falls back to touching a `data/FREEZE` sentinel file if the server is unresponsive). While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. The break-glass procedure is: SSH into the server host first, then run `adc freeze` / `adc restore-artifacts` there.\n/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md:Proposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_024c4c6d164c48658113bfea","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n28\t                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n29\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n30\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n31\t             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n32\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n33\t      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n34\t              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n35\t  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n36\t      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n37\t      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n38\t  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n39\t      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n40\t```\n41\t\n42\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n43\t\n44\t> The ~500ms and ~1-2s figures above — and every per-stage number in this plan — are design **estimates**. Nothing on the hot path has been measured yet. See Latency Budget & Instrumentation.\n45\t\n46\t### Latency Budget & Instrumentation\n47\t\n48\tThe <3s promise is the product's central claim, so it gets a budget, instrumentation, and a gate — not just a diagram annotation. Per-stage targets below are estimates chosen so the end-to-end budget is plausible; the Measured columns show actual captured timings from 206 production-like runs across three test shapes (see bead adc-2xf52 for detailed analysis).\n49\t\n50\t| Stage | Target (ESTIMATE) | Measured p50 | Measured p95 |\n51\t|-------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|\n52\t| STT final transcript (Web Speech API) | ~300ms | *Not measured* (client-side) | *Not measured* (client-side) |\n53\t| Intent Router (haiku-class via ZAI proxy) | ~500ms | **1,587-2,074ms** ❌ | **2,527-4,301ms** ❌ |\n54\t| Fetch — first source returns (surfaces as a per-source progress state on the pending card) | ~500ms | **0-16ms** ✅ | **0-21ms** ✅ |\n55\t| Fetch — window closes (all sources resolved or timed out; gates synthesize start — see Fetch Strand) | ~1s | **0-51ms** ✅ | **0-191ms** ✅ |\n56\t| Synthesize — first token (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy; starts at fetch-window close) | ~1s (cap set by the e2e gate — see internal-consistency note) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) |\n57\t| Synthesize — total (first token through completion) | ~1-2s | **3,108-3,984ms** ❌ | **4,663-7,877ms** ❌ |\n58\t| SSE emit → first card render | ~100ms | *Not measured* | *Not measured* |\n59\t| Escalate — bead formulation + safety validation + `bf create` (haiku-class via ZAI proxy; off the first-card path, see note below) | ~2s | **3,992ms** ❌ | **5,445ms** ❌ |\n60\t| **End-to-end: utterance end → first partial card** | **< 3s** | **5,219-5,571ms** ❌ | **8,853-10,404ms** ❌ |\n61\t\n62\t**Internal consistency — the e2e row is the binding gate.** The hot-path stages are strictly sequential (STT → router → fetch-window close → synthesize first token → SSE emit), so their targets must sum inside the end-to-end budget: 300 + 500 + 1000 + 1000 + 100 ms ≈ 2.9s against the 3s gate, leaving ~100ms of unallocated slack. (The fetch first-source and escalate rows sit off this critical path — the former lands inside the fetch window, the latter is off the first-card path per the note below.) Synthesize-first-token was originally sketched at ~1–2s — the Hot Path diagram's ~1–2s figure survives as the estimate for the *full* synthesize call, first token through completion — but at a 2s first token the stages sum to ~3.9s: every stage could pass its own budget individually while the end-to-end gate fails, a contradiction that would otherwise surface only at rehearsal. Hence the ~1s first-token allocation above. All per-stage targets remain ESTIMATES and are **provisional allocations, not independent pass/fail gates**: once the Measured p50/p95 columns fill, re-derive the stage allocations from measured slack (a stage running under budget donates headroom to one running over) with the e2e row held fixed and overriding — the Gate below then applies against the re-derived allocations. A run where every stage meets its allocation but the e2e row misses is still a failed run.\n63\t\n64\t**Task-profile first card.** The escalate stage does not gate the <3s promise: for a task-profile thread, the ack/pending card (a served built-in template, not a component-library render — see Component Library → Built-in cards) renders as soon as the router resolves the thread (router + SSE emit only) — before bead formulation and `bf create` complete — then updates in place with the bead ref when the escalate stage returns. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n65\t\n66\t**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). This is not optional telemetry: it is the only way the Measured columns get filled, and it doubles as the per-step timing log the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist requires.\n67\t\n68\t**Gate.** The demo cannot be scheduled until the Measured p50/p95 columns are filled from real runs (rehearsal timing logs count). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n69\t\n70\t### The Async Path\n71\t\n72\t```\n73\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n74\t  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n75\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n76\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n77\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n78\t  → Result written to session store results table\n79\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Until ADR-1 lands, a result arriving with no active canvas is persisted to the session store but reaches no surface.\n83\t\n84\tThe diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n85\t\n86\t- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n87\t- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n88\t- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n89\t\n90\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n91\t\n92\t```\n93\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n94\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n95\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML)\n96\t      generates update\n97\t      surfaces diff to user\n98\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n99\t```\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8fe98516-420b-4f80-87c3-e48d571e6ede","uuid":"d124b256-deb3-4891-a07f-f4745df52984","timestamp":"2026-07-24T01:09:07.949Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n\n> The ~500ms and ~1-2s figures above — and every per-stage number in this plan — are design **estimates**. Nothing on the hot path has been measured yet. See Latency Budget & Instrumentation.\n\n### Latency Budget & Instrumentation\n\nThe <3s promise is the product's central claim, so it gets a budget, instrumentation, and a gate — not just a diagram annotation. Per-stage targets below are estimates chosen so the end-to-end budget is plausible; the Measured columns show actual captured timings from 206 production-like runs across three test shapes (see bead adc-2xf52 for detailed analysis).\n\n| Stage | Target (ESTIMATE) | Measured p50 | Measured p95 |\n|-------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|\n| STT final transcript (Web Speech API) | ~300ms | *Not measured* (client-side) | *Not measured* (client-side) |\n| Intent Router (haiku-class via ZAI proxy) | ~500ms | **1,587-2,074ms** ❌ | **2,527-4,301ms** ❌ |\n| Fetch — first source returns (surfaces as a per-source progress state on the pending card) | ~500ms | **0-16ms** ✅ | **0-21ms** ✅ |\n| Fetch — window closes (all sources resolved or timed out; gates synthesize start — see Fetch Strand) | ~1s | **0-51ms** ✅ | **0-191ms** ✅ |\n| Synthesize — first token (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy; starts at fetch-window close) | ~1s (cap set by the e2e gate — see internal-consistency note) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) |\n| Synthesize — total (first token through completion) | ~1-2s | **3,108-3,984ms** ❌ | **4,663-7,877ms** ❌ |\n| SSE emit → first card render | ~100ms | *Not measured* | *Not measured* |\n| Escalate — bead formulation + safety validation + `bf create` (haiku-class via ZAI proxy; off the first-card path, see note below) | ~2s | **3,992ms** ❌ | **5,445ms** ❌ |\n| **End-to-end: utterance end → first partial card** | **< 3s** | **5,219-5,571ms** ❌ | **8,853-10,404ms** ❌ |\n\n**Internal consistency — the e2e row is the binding gate.** The hot-path stages are strictly sequential (STT → router → fetch-window close → synthesize first token → SSE emit), so their targets must sum inside the end-to-end budget: 300 + 500 + 1000 + 1000 + 100 ms ≈ 2.9s against the 3s gate, leaving ~100ms of unallocated slack. (The fetch first-source and escalate rows sit off this critical path — the former lands inside the fetch window, the latter is off the first-card path per the note below.) Synthesize-first-token was originally sketched at ~1–2s — the Hot Path diagram's ~1–2s figure survives as the estimate for the *full* synthesize call, first token through completion — but at a 2s first token the stages sum to ~3.9s: every stage could pass its own budget individually while the end-to-end gate fails, a contradiction that would otherwise surface only at rehearsal. Hence the ~1s first-token allocation above. All per-stage targets remain ESTIMATES and are **provisional allocations, not independent pass/fail gates**: once the Measured p50/p95 columns fill, re-derive the stage allocations from measured slack (a stage running under budget donates headroom to one running over) with the e2e row held fixed and overriding — the Gate below then applies against the re-derived allocations. A run where every stage meets its allocation but the e2e row misses is still a failed run.\n\n**Task-profile first card.** The escalate stage does not gate the <3s promise: for a task-profile thread, the ack/pending card (a served built-in template, not a component-library render — see Component Library → Built-in cards) renders as soon as the router resolves the thread (router + SSE emit only) — before bead formulation and `bf create` complete — then updates in place with the bead ref when the escalate stage returns. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n\n**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). This is not optional telemetry: it is the only way the Measured columns get filled, and it doubles as the per-step timing log the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist requires.\n\n**Gate.** The demo cannot be scheduled until the Measured p50/p95 columns are filled from real runs (rehearsal timing logs count). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Until ADR-1 lands, a result arriving with no active canvas is persisted to the session store but reaches no surface.\n\nThe diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n\n- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML)\n      generates update\n      surfaces diff to user\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n```\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1213}}}

The hot-path server also writes to that DB on every dispatch, but only mechanical render/usage state (`card_cache` rows and the usage-stat columns), never definitions. One flow, two agents, disjoint write scopes — see Security Model.\n102\t\n103\t---\n104\t\n105\t## Components\n106\t\n107\t### 1. Intent Router\n108\t\n109\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n110\t\n111\tOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n112\t\n113\t```json\n114\t[\n115\t  {\n116\t    \"project_slug\": \"pbx-web\",\n117\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n118\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n119\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the pbx web caught up?\"\n120\t  },\n121\t  {\n122\t    \"project_slug\": \"whisper-stt\",\n123\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n124\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n125\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of whisper stt\"\n126\t  },\n127\t  {\n128\t    \"project_slug\": \"whisper-stt\",\n129\t    \"intent_type\": \"lookup\",\n130\t    \"lookup_kind\": \"logs\",\n131\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n132\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"and pull up its recent logs\"\n133\t  }\n134\t]\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t`lookup_kind` appears only on `lookup` threads (see the intent-type list below); no other intent type carries it.\n138\t\n139\tIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n140\t\n141\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n142\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create) — executes only through the Action Execution Model (declarative-config Git operations + ArgoCD sync status, or reviewed escalation beads); never direct kubectl mutation\n143\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n144\t- **lookup**: Find specific information. Every lookup thread carries a router-emitted `lookup_kind` — `logs` | `config` | `docs` (default `docs` when the utterance names nothing sharper) — because \"recent logs\" and \"deployment config\" for the same project are different fetches and different cards, not one. `lookup_kind` selects the fetch matrix (`prompts/fetch/lookup-{lookup_kind}.md`) and is embedded in the result's `result_type` (`lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}`, e.g. `lookup:logs:whisper-stt` vs `lookup:config:whisper-stt`), so log-lookup and config-lookup results select components independently. Persisted on the intent row (`intents.lookup_kind`)\n145\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders — **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED**: no reminders table, scheduler, or module exists. The router does not dispatch this type; a reminder-shaped utterance is handled as `clarification` with a \"reminders aren't available yet\" card. Minimal design sketch in Future Work\n146\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n147\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n148\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n149\t- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n150\t\n151\tThe router reloads its segmentation prompt and the project registry per call through an mtime-checked cache: each file is stat'd on every call and re-read only when its mtime has changed — the same strategy every hot-reloaded artifact uses (see Hot-Reload Architecture). Hot-reload is automatic; an edit takes effect on the next call.\n152\t\n153\t**Urgency ownership:** the router assigns the initial `urgency` on every intent thread as part of hot-path segmentation, guided by `prompts/urgency.md` (folded into the single router call — no extra LLM call on the hot path). The escalate strand refines urgency when formulating task-profile beads, and rule-driven ambient-monitoring results — which have no intent thread behind them — take their urgency from the firing rule in `config/monitoring.yaml` (see Bead Watcher). No other component assigns urgency; in particular, synthesize never does — it outputs `{data, summary}` and the thread's router-assigned urgency carries through to the result unchanged.\n154\t\n155\tAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n156\t\n157\t### 2. Project Registry\n158\t\n159\t**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n160\t\n161\tDefines projects the router knows about:\n162\t\n163\t```yaml\n164\tprojects:\n165\t  pbx-web:\n166\t    aliases: [\"pbx\", \"the pbx\", \"phone system\"]\n167\t    description: \"PBX web interface on ardenone-cluster\"\n168\t    cluster: ardenone-cluster  # ArgoCD endpoint for this cluster resolves via config/clusters.yaml (see Fetch Strand — Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution)\n169\t    namespace: pbx-web\n170\t    repo_path: /home/coding/declarative-config  # local checkout: git-log + per-project bead listing (pbx-web manifests live in k8s/ardenone-cluster/pbx-web/)\n171\t    argocd_app: pbx-web  # ArgoCD Application name for the fetch matrix's sync row; defaults to the project slug when omitted\n172\t    sla_hours: 6          # pending-card SLA override (see The Async Path — Visible aging)\n173\t    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm, lookup, task-profile]\n174\t    # `action` appears in intent_support for routing completeness, but action\n175\t    # dispatch is DISABLED until the Action strand ships — see Action Execution\n176\t    # Model (Status: executor NOT BUILT)\n177\t    workflows:\n178\t      deploy:\n179\t        # DESIGN-ONLY — no executor interprets `steps` yet; the step vocabulary\n180\t        # is defined in Action Execution Model → \"Future: Action strand\".\n181\t        # Mutating steps follow the Action Execution Model: gitops-commit\n182\t        # edits jedarden/declarative-config; argocd-sync-status polls the\n183\t        # read-only ArgoCD API until the app reports Synced/Healthy\n184\t        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, gitops-commit, argocd-sync-status, pod-status]\n185\t\n186\t  whisper-stt:\n187\t    aliases: [\"whisper\", \"stt\", \"speech-to-text\"]\n188\t    description: \"Whisper STT service on ardenone-cluster\"\n189\t    cluster: ardenone-cluster\n190\t    namespace: whisper-stt\n191\t    repo_path: /home/coding/declarative-config  # local checkout: git-log + per-project bead listing (whisper-stt manifests live in k8s/ardenone-cluster/whisper-stt/)\n192\t    argocd_app: whisper-stt  # explicit here for clarity; equals the slug default\n193\t    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup, task-profile]\n194\t```\n195\t\n196\tUpdated by the self-modification agent. `src/environment/discovery.py` (the repo scanner) only *seeds* the registry — an initial or explicitly re-run scan that proposes entries; after seeding, the self-modification agent is the sole ongoing author, and the scanner never overwrites an entry the agent has written. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n197\t\n198\t### 3. The hot-path server also writes to that DB on every dispatch, but only mechanical render/usage state (`card_cache` rows and the usage-stat columns), never definitions. One flow, two agents, disjoint write scopes — see Security Model.\n\n---\n\n## Components\n\n### 1. Intent Router\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"pbx-web\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the pbx web caught up?\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"whisper-stt\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of whisper stt\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"whisper-stt\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"lookup\",\n    \"lookup_kind\": \"logs\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"and pull up its recent logs\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n`lookup_kind` appears only on `lookup` threads (see the intent-type list below); no other intent type carries it.\n\nIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create) — executes only through the Action Execution Model (declarative-config Git operations + ArgoCD sync status, or reviewed escalation beads); never direct kubectl mutation\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information. Every lookup thread carries a router-emitted `lookup_kind` — `logs` | `config` | `docs` (default `docs` when the utterance names nothing sharper) — because \"recent logs\" and \"deployment config\" for the same project are different fetches and different cards, not one. `lookup_kind` selects the fetch matrix (`prompts/fetch/lookup-{lookup_kind}.md`) and is embedded in the result's `result_type` (`lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}`, e.g. `lookup:logs:whisper-stt` vs `lookup:config:whisper-stt`), so log-lookup and config-lookup results select components independently. Persisted on the intent row (`intents.lookup_kind`)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders — **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED**: no reminders table, scheduler, or module exists. The router does not dispatch this type; a reminder-shaped utterance is handled as `clarification` with a \"reminders aren't available yet\" card. Minimal design sketch in Future Work\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n\nThe router reloads its segmentation prompt and the project registry per call through an mtime-checked cache: each file is stat'd on every call and re-read only when its mtime has changed — the same strategy every hot-reloaded artifact uses (see Hot-Reload Architecture). Hot-reload is automatic; an edit takes effect on the next call.\n\n**Urgency ownership:** the router assigns the initial `urgency` on every intent thread as part of hot-path segmentation, guided by `prompts/urgency.md` (folded into the single router call — no extra LLM call on the hot path). The escalate strand refines urgency when formulating task-profile beads, and rule-driven ambient-monitoring results — which have no intent thread behind them — take their urgency from the firing rule in `config/monitoring.yaml` (see Bead Watcher). No other component assigns urgency; in particular, synthesize never does — it outputs `{data, summary}` and the thread's router-assigned urgency carries through to the result unchanged.\n\nAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n\n### 2. Project Registry\n\n**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n\nDefines projects the router knows about:\n\n```yaml\nprojects:\n  pbx-web:\n    aliases: [\"pbx\", \"the pbx\", \"phone system\"]\n    description: \"PBX web interface on ardenone-cluster\"\n    cluster: ardenone-cluster  # ArgoCD endpoint for this cluster resolves via config/clusters.yaml (see Fetch Strand — Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution)\n    namespace: pbx-web\n    repo_path: /home/coding/declarative-config  # local checkout: git-log + per-project bead listing (pbx-web manifests live in k8s/ardenone-cluster/pbx-web/)\n    argocd_app: pbx-web  # ArgoCD Application name for the fetch matrix's sync row; defaults to the project slug when omitted\n    sla_hours: 6          # pending-card SLA override (see The Async Path — Visible aging)\n    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm, lookup, task-profile]\n    # `action` appears in intent_support for routing completeness, but action\n    # dispatch is DISABLED until the Action strand ships — see Action Execution\n    # Model (Status: executor NOT BUILT)\n    workflows:\n      deploy:\n        # DESIGN-ONLY — no executor interprets `steps` yet; the step vocabulary\n        # is defined in Action Execution Model → \"Future: Action strand\".\n        # Mutating steps follow the Action Execution Model: gitops-commit\n        # edits jedarden/declarative-config; argocd-sync-status polls the\n        # read-only ArgoCD API until the app reports Synced/Healthy\n        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, gitops-commit, argocd-sync-status, pod-status]\n\n  whisper-stt:\n    aliases: [\"whisper\", \"stt\", \"speech-to-text\"]\n    description: \"Whisper STT service on ardenone-cluster\"\n    cluster: ardenone-cluster\n    namespace: whisper-stt\n    repo_path: /home/coding/declarative-config  # local checkout: git-log + per-project bead listing (whisper-stt manifests live in k8s/ardenone-cluster/whisper-stt/)\n    argocd_app: whisper-stt  # explicit here for clarity; equals the slug default\n    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup, task-profile]\n```\n\nUpdated by the self-modification agent. `src/environment/discovery.py` (the repo scanner) only *seeds* the registry — an initial or explicitly re-run scan that proposes entries; after seeding, the self-modification agent is the sole ongoing author, and the scanner never overwrites an entry the agent has written. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n\n### 3. Fetch Strand\n","numLines":100,"startLine":100,"totalLines":1213}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_288f85c327914d0c81cb28f1","type":"tool_result","content":"336-- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n337-- Versioning is git: every artifact write (`prompts/*.md`, `config/*.yaml`) is a git commit with a machine-generated message; rolling back any update is one instruction → `git revert` of that commit. Component library artifacts get the same guarantee through the `component_versions` table. No bespoke version store.\n338:- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n339:- Out-of-band kill switch: self-modification can break the very interface used to undo it (a bad router prompt means no more routed instructions). Two escape hatches bypass the interface entirely: `ADC_SELFMOD_FREEZE=1` in the server environment, or `adc freeze` from the CLI (which falls back to touching a `data/FREEZE` sentinel file if the server is unresponsive). While frozen, every self-modification and auto-apply write is refused, and `adc restore-artifacts` git-reverts `prompts/` and `config/` to the last-known-good commit — no canvas, voice session, or router involvement required. **Break-glass caveat:** the sentinel-file fallback and the git revert are local filesystem/git operations against the server's artifact store — `adc` is a thin HTTP client (see adc CLI connectivity), so from a remote machine neither works exactly when the server is unresponsive. The break-glass procedure is: SSH into the server host first, then run `adc freeze` / `adc restore-artifacts` there.\n340-\n341:### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n342-\n343-**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n344-\n345-Runs on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n346-\n347-Examples:\n348-- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n349-- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n350-- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n351-\n352:Proposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold — and an auto-apply is not a third artifact writer: the accepted proposal executes through the self-modification agent's write path, inheriting its write scope (`prompts/` and `config/` only), its git-commit-per-write rollback guarantee (one-instruction revert), and the out-of-band kill switch — all auto-apply is refused while the freeze is engaged.\n353-\n354-### 10. Bead Watcher\n355-\n356:**Runtime:** In-process daemon loop — an asyncio background task spawned by FastAPI lifespan startup, inside the same process as the server (see Deploy Stage A process model). No LLM\n357-\n358-Watches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. Detection is CLI-only: the watcher polls `bf list --status closed` on a fixed interval (default 30s) and keeps a high-water mark (newest close timestamp already processed) to pick out new closures. On the same tick it checks each open tracked bead — every unresolved `intents.bead_ref` — via `bf show`, parsing comments past a per-bead high-water mark for `REFUSED:` entries (the circuit-breaker signal; see The Async Path). All `bf` invocations run from the aide-de-camp repo's beads workspace (see Beads-Workspace Scoping under the Escalate Strand). It never reads `.beads/` files directly — the SQLite db is the CLI's private store, and `issues.jsonl` is only a flush checkpoint that misses unflushed mutations; both are documented corruption/staleness footguns in this workspace.\n359-\n360-On close:\n361-1. Resolves the closed bead to its intent via `intents.bead_ref` and reads `session_id` from the intent row — the bead itself carries no session metadata; the mapping lives entirely in the session store\n362-2. Looks up active surface in session store\n363-3. Writes result to `results` table and marks the intent `resolved` (or `failed`, per The Async Path's terminal-failure rule)\n364-4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n365-\n366-> ⚠ The Telegram push in step 4 is NON-FUNCTIONAL until ADR-1 lands — `src/telegram/fallback.py`'s delivery methods are stubs pointed at an unreachable bridge URL (see ADR-1). Today, a bead closing with no active canvas writes to `results` but notifies no one.\n--\n643-│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n644-│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n645:│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n646-│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n647-│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n648:│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n649-│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n650-│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\n651-│   │   └── orchestrator.py      ← FetchStrand: concurrent fetch execution, streaming, coverage tracking\n652-│   ├── intent/              ← intent router (LLM classification)\n653-│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\n654-│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction (supporting store for cross-session context; not yet surfaced by any component — Future Work)\n655-│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n656-│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n657-│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n658-│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n--\n726-Running command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n727-\n728:**Stage A process model — exactly one process, by design.** The command above is the complete process inventory. The bead watcher (which also hosts ambient monitoring and the async-path circuit breaker — see Bead Watcher) is **not** a second command an operator must remember: FastAPI's lifespan startup spawns the watcher loop as an asyncio background task inside this same uvicorn process. A separately documented daemon is exactly the step that gets skipped, and its absence is silent — closures never surface, SLAs never fire, monitoring never ticks, while every test still passes (the \"verified-in-tests, dead live\" pattern). Startup-spawning makes that failure impossible; liveness is still checked, not assumed: `GET /health` reports `watcher.alive` / `watcher.last_tick_at` (see Bead Watcher), and the pre-demo seeding runbook verifies it in item (3).\n729-\n730-No container, no CI, no ArgoCD.
